content == {{Shrine | Name=Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine | Image=[[file:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} Akao 42 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 32 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 9 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} Akao 42 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 32 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 9 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

content == {{Shrine | Name=Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine | Image=[[file:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} Akao 42 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 32 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 9 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

content == {{Shrine | Name=Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine | Image=[[file:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} Akao 42 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 32 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 9 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

content == {{Shrine | Name=Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine | Image=[[file:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} Akao 42 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 32 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 9 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

content == {{Shrine | Name=Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine | Image=[[file:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} Akao 42 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 32 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 9 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine status={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Main sanctuary=None | Sacred object={{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} | Founded=Unknown | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}} | Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Oshisakayamaguchinimasu Jinja Kusunoki.jpg|thumb|220px|Giant camphor tree (designated cultural property of Sakurai City)]] '''Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine''' (おしさかやまぐちにますじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Sakurai City|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines within {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|WD=Q1760119|1=Norito}}—namely, {{ill|lt=Asukaniimasu Shrine|Q11665959|WD=Q11665959|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}}, Iwaki, Oshisaka, Hase, {{ill|lt=Unebi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11610732|WD=Q11610732|lt=Miminashi|1=Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine}}—and is one of the eighty-five shrines of the rain-prayer deities. === Enshrined deity === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' Descriptions regarding the identity of the enshrined deity are as follows: * *Yamato Meisho Zue*: “Currently called the deity Ten’ichi.” * *Yamato-shi*: “Now called Ten’ichi no Kami.” * *Shūshōgatsu Kanjō Shinmeichō*: “The Ten’ichi Daimyōjin of this place.” * *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō*: “Enshrined deity: Ten’ichi Shinmei Daimyōjin.” * *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*: “Enshrined deity: the mountain god Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto (omitted)… currently called Ten’ichi.” * *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*: “Enshrined deity: Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami.” === History === === Overview === The date of founding is unknown. A record concerning Oshisaka Shrine estates appears in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|lt=Great Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} Shōzeichō*. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}), one household of {{ill|lt=shrine land|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} was donated (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the monthly and Niiname festivals. According to an on-site guide (Sakurai City Tourism Association), in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1397|Q6410|WD=Q6410|1=1397}}), when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinkaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} was constructed, a giant camphor tree sacred to this shrine was felled and used for the ceiling boards. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as “Ten’ichi Daimyōjin.” In the 37th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1904|Q2046|WD=Q2046|1=1904}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Court rank === * On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*). The notation used is “Oshisaka Yamaguchi Shrine.” === Precincts === Immediately to the left upon entering the precincts stands a giant camphor tree with a circumference of 7.3 meters, a height of 25 meters, and a height of 2 meters from the base. It is estimated to be over 500 years old and is designated as a cultural property of Sakurai City. === Access === * Approximately 800 meters southeast from {{ill|lt=Yamato-Asakura Station|Q8047967|WD=Q8047967|1=Yamato-Asakura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === References === * On-site information board === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A3%E5%9D%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22707/ Oshisaka Yamaguchi-ni-masu Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Yamaguchi Numasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
